category_reason: system/internal signal or no positively user-visible signal (expected All 26 'measure api consumer satisfaction' scenario checks pass against the documented behaviour, observed [api-tester-measure-api-consumer-satisfaction] quality outcome=FAIL; metric=0.0)
sub_test: Aggregate failure: measure api consumer satisfaction (26/26 scenario checks failed)
title: [measure-api-consumer-satisfaction] Aggregate failure: measure api consumer satisfaction (26/26 scenario checks failed) on n/a — aggregate finding across endpoints — expected All 26 'measure api consumer satisfaction' scenario checks pass against the documented behaviour, observed [api-tester-measure-api-consumer-satisfaction] quality outcome=FAIL; metric=0.0 (undocumented)
expected: All 26 'measure api consumer satisfaction' scenario checks pass against the documented behaviour

testing_steps: 1. Start the target API at http://localhost:8899.

2. Write a script that samples every endpoint in agent-foundry/data/openapi.json (~5 requests each) against http://localhost:8899, recording latency, error rate and schema-consistency, and computes the satisfaction score per the evidence file's formula.
3. Assert the score meets the pass threshold; sub-threshold endpoints locate the failure.
4. Compare your script's failures with the per-scenario evidence in TestCases/measure-api-consumer-satisfaction/cases.json — this run recorded 26 of 26 checks failing.

description: The measure-api-consumer-satisfaction check failed in aggregate against http://localhost:8899: [api-tester-measure-api-consumer-satisfaction] quality outcome=FAIL; metric=0.0. The per-scenario evidence is in TestCases/measure-api-consumer-satisfaction/cases.json. There is no single reproducible API call — reproduce it with the script described in the steps below (which is also why this bug has no Postman section).
steps_to_reproduce: 1. Start the target API at http://localhost:8899.

2. Write a script that samples every endpoint in agent-foundry/data/openapi.json (~5 requests each) against http://localhost:8899, recording latency, error rate and schema-consistency, and computes the satisfaction score per the evidence file's formula.
3. Assert the score meets the pass threshold; sub-threshold endpoints locate the failure.
4. Compare your script's failures with the per-scenario evidence in TestCases/measure-api-consumer-satisfaction/cases.json — this run recorded 26 of 26 checks failing.

documentation.note: No public documentation states this expected behavior (documentation-reviewer verdict: missing-docs). The expectation derives from the universal HTTP/REST contract (agent-foundry/references/contract-oracle.md).
